title: croton vaughanii
intro: Croton vaughanii, also known as the Croton or Wild Forest, is a small, deciduous tree native to the tropical forests of Malaysia and parts of Southeast Asia. Traditionally, the plant's roots and leaves have been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Croton vaughanii include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and improving digestion, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically dried and then used to make tea, infusions, or extracts, which can be taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
